message:
feat(btree): deferred delete with freeblock reuse and in-place leaf insert

Overhaul the B-tree write path to defer costly rebalancing operations
and reuse freed space directly, significantly reducing write
amplification for table cursors.

Deferred delete (table cursors only):
- New remove_table_cell_from_leaf_deferred: creates a freeblock from
  the deleted cell's space instead of immediately rewriting/compacting
  the page, properly chaining freeblocks and handling fragment bytes
  for cells < 4 bytes
- Index cursors retain the existing remove + immediate balance behavior
- New collapse_empty_table_root_if_needed: lazily rewrites an interior
  root with zero total rows back to an empty leaf
- first()/last() now call collapse when navigation finds no rows

In-place leaf insert optimization:
- Rewritten try_insert_on_leaf: modifies the in-memory StackEntry
  directly (header, cell pointers, page bytes) instead of read-modify-
  write-reload cycle, eliminating an extra page read per leaf insert
- New try_allocate_table_leaf_freeblock: walks the freeblock linked
  list to find a slot large enough for a new cell, enabling insert-
  into-freespace without compaction or balance
- New compact_current_table_leaf: full leaf page compaction that reads
  all cells, rewrites them contiguously, zeroes freeblocks
- table_insert loop now attempts compaction before falling back to
  balance_for_insert when try_insert_on_leaf returns false

Empty-leaf traversal fix:
- move_to_leftmost/rightmost_leaf: when hitting a leaf with cell_count
  == 0, now advances past the empty leaf instead of stopping at EOF,
  fixing scans that stopped prematurely after deferred deletes

Instrumentation:
- New BtreeLeafReuseSnapshot with no_split_reuse_hits,
  conservative_reload_fallbacks, page_header_rebuild_count counters
- Hot-path depth gauge recording on every btree_op
- Page reload instrumentation via record_page_header_rebuild

Validation relaxed: empty non-root leaves and empty interior divider
cells now tolerated (consistent with deferred-delete semantics)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
